description: The IT Act provides legal framework for electronic commerce, digital signatures, and cybercrime in India. It was amended in 2008 to address emerging cyber threats.

key_provisions.43: Penalty for damage to computer system
key_provisions.43A: Compensation for failure to protect data (added 2008)
key_provisions.66A: Offensive messages (STRUCK DOWN by SC in 2015)
key_provisions.69A: Power to block access to websites
key_provisions.72A: Disclosure of information in breach of contract

important_judgments.case: Shreya Singhal v. Union of India (2015)
important_judgments.holding: Section 66A struck down as unconstitutional. Section 69A and 79 read down to require court/government order for content removal.

important_judgments.case: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017)
important_judgments.holding: Right to privacy declared fundamental right — impacts data protection under IT Act.
